description_long: This is a military-style twin-engine airplane with a dark green camouflage paint scheme and a streamlined fuselage. The wings are straight and mounted mid-fuselage, with two prominent engine nacelles located beneath each wing. The cockpit is positioned at the very front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, featuring multiple window panels. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration, with a nose wheel and two main wheels under the wings.
description: A dark green, twin-engine military airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a forward enclosed cockpit, and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, mid-mounted wings, and the forward cockpit with a clear canopy.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin engine nacelles under the wings, the nose wheel, and the cockpit windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail section, engine placement, and the overall streamlined profile.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the underwing engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted under each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main wheels under the wings.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple window panels, likely multi-seat.
